agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Integration request to couple Sales/Order Management with Warehouse Management for real-time stock validation at order placement time. This fundamentally changes the relationship between two currently independent bounded contexts.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration: Warehouse Management ↔ Sales/Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"StockLevel","definition":"Non-negative integer representing current physical inventory of a Product in the Warehouse context"},{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's intent to purchase one or more products, owned by the Sales context"},{"term":"OrderLine","definition":"A single line item within an Order, referencing a Product ID and desired Quantity"},{"term":"Stock Reservation","definition":"A tentative claim on inventory that reduces available stock without physically picking items — currently missing from both contexts and required for this feature"},{"term":"Available Stock","definition":"StockLevel minus all outstanding Stock Reservations — the quantity actually available for new orders. Distinct from physical StockLevel."},{"term":"Blocked Order","definition":"An Order that has been rejected or held due to insufficient available stock at placement time"},{"term":"Stock Available Indicator","definition":"A read-model projection showing the sales team whether a product has sufficient available stock, derived from Warehouse data"}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"StockLevel cannot go below zero","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Represents physical reality — you cannot pick items that do not exist. Violating this means the system's inventory model is corrupt.","testable_assertion":"For every Product, StockLevel >= 0 at all times, across all concurrent operations"},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An Order must be blocked if Available Stock (StockLevel minus outstanding reservations) is less than the requested Quantity at the time of placement","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy decision. A VP could decide to allow backorders, partial fulfillment, or waitlisting instead of blocking. The blocking behavior is a policy, not a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a Product with Available Stock of N, when an Order is placed for Quantity > N, then the Order status is set to 'Blocked'"},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Stock validation must use Available Stock (StockLevel minus reservations), not raw StockLevel","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Using raw StockLevel allows double-selling: two concurrent orders could each see StockLevel=5 and both succeed for Quantity=5, resulting in oversell. Available Stock is the only correct basis for availability decisions.","testable_assertion":"Available Stock = StockLevel - SUM(outstanding reservation quantities) for a given Product"},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A successful order placement must create a Stock Reservation to prevent overselling between placement and picking","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without reservations, there is a race window between order acceptance and physical pick that allows overselling. This is a data consistency invariant.","testable_assertion":"When an Order is accepted, a corresponding Reservation is created atomically, and Available Stock is decremented before the operation returns"},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The 'stock available' indicator shown to the Sales team is eventually consistent and must not be used as the authoritative check for order blocking","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Real-time display is a UX preference. The authoritative stock check happens at order placement. The indicator is a read-model convenience.","testable_assertion":"The stock indicator may lag behind actual Available Stock, but order placement always performs a consistent check against the Warehouse context"},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Context B (Sales) must not directly mutate Context A (Warehouse) state — stock validation crosses contexts via a well-defined integration mechanism (domain events, anti-corruption layer, or synchronous query)","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Bounded context autonomy is a structural invariant. If Sales directly writes to Warehouse tables, context boundaries are destroyed, leading to coupled deployments, shared schema drift, and loss of independent evolvability.","testable_assertion":"No Sales context code path directly modifies StockLevel or any Warehouse-owned aggregate"}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Product with Available Stock of 10","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for Quantity 5 of that Product","then":"The Order is accepted, a Stock Reservation of 5 is created, and Available Stock for the Product becomes 5"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Product with Available Stock of 3","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for Quantity 5 of that Product","then":"The Order is blocked with a clear reason indicating insufficient stock, and no Reservation is created"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"Two customers concurrently place Orders for the same Product with Available Stock of 5, each requesting Quantity 5","when":"Both orders are processed","then":"Exactly one Order is accepted and one is blocked. Available Stock never goes negative. No overselling occurs."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A Product with Available Stock of 10","when":"The sales team views the product in their interface","then":"A stock available indicator is displayed reflecting the most recent known Available Stock"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An Order has been blocked due to insufficient stock","when":"New stock is received from a supplier, increasing Available Stock above the blocked Order's required Quantity","then":"The system does NOT automatically unblock the Order (unless this behavior is explicitly specified — see escalations)"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An accepted Order's Reservation exists","when":"The Order is cancelled by the customer before picking","then":"The Reservation is released and Available Stock increases by the reserved Quantity"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An Order with multiple OrderLines spanning different Products","when":"One product has sufficient stock but another does not","then":"The entire Order is blocked (atomic order acceptance — partial acceptance policy is unspecified, see escalations)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent orders exhaust stock simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Only orders that can be fully reserved are accepted; the rest are blocked. This requires serialized or optimistic-concurrency stock checks to prevent race conditions."},{"scenario":"Stock received (supplier delivery) while orders are being placed","expected_behavior":"Incoming stock increases StockLevel and Available Stock. Orders in flight should see updated availability if not yet committed."},{"scenario":"Order cancelled after reservation but before pick","expected_behavior":"Reservation is released, Available Stock restored. Previously blocked orders are NOT automatically retried unless policy says otherwise."},{"scenario":"Product exists in Sales context but not yet in Warehouse context","expected_behavior":"Order should be blocked or rejected with a clear error — not silently accepted with no stock check."},{"scenario":"Stock indicator shows 'available' but stock is reserved between indicator refresh and order placement","expected_behavior":"Order is correctly blocked at placement time. The indicator was stale — this is expected eventual consistency, not a bug."},{"scenario":"Extremely large order quantity exceeding any reasonable stock level","expected_behavior":"System handles gracefully — same blocking logic applies. No integer overflow or special handling unless business defines a max order quantity."},{"scenario":"Reservation expires or times out","expected_behavior":"Policy not specified. If reservations are time-bounded, expired reservations must release stock. This needs explicit business rules."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Prevents overselling, which avoids costly fulfillment failures, refunds, and customer compensation. However, overly aggressive blocking may reject orders that could have been fulfilled (e.g., if stock is in transit). The reservation mechanism adds operational overhead but protects revenue integrity.","operational":"Introduces a synchronous or near-synchronous dependency between Sales and Warehouse contexts. If the Warehouse service is down, order placement may be blocked entirely — this is a significant availability concern. Requires new infrastructure: reservation tracking, stock projection read models, and cross-context communication (events or API).","user":"Sales team gains visibility into stock levels, reducing manual checking and customer disappointment. Customers get immediate feedback on order viability instead of post-placement cancellation emails. However, the 'real-time' indicator introduces a false sense of precision — eventual consistency must be communicated clearly in UX.","risk":"Primary risk is temporal coupling: Sales becomes dependent on Warehouse availability. Secondary risk is the reservation mechanism — without TTL/expiry, zombie reservations can lock up inventory permanently. Third risk is the read-model indicator lagging behind reality, leading to user frustration when displayed availability doesn't match order outcomes.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Better experience — immediate feedback on stock availability at order time instead of delayed cancellation. Possible frustration if indicator shows 'available' but order is blocked."},{"group":"Sales Team","impact":"Real-time stock visibility reduces manual lookups and phone calls to warehouse. Must understand indicator is approximate, not guaranteed."},{"group":"Warehouse Team","impact":"New reservation concept changes how they view available inventory. Must account for reserved-but-not-yet-picked stock in their workflows."},{"group":"Engineering / Platform","impact":"Must implement cross-context integration without violating bounded context boundaries. Significant architectural decision: synchronous call vs. domain events vs. saga."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Reduced refunds and compensation from overselling. Potential lost revenue from false blocks during high-concurrency sales events."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec is underspecified in several critical areas: (1) no mention of reservations, which are essential to correctness, (2) 'automatically block' is ambiguous — does it mean reject, hold for review, or queue? (3) 'real time' indicator has no consistency guarantees defined, (4) no policy on partial fulfillment, (5) no policy on what happens to blocked orders when stock arrives, (6) no discussion of the coupling/availability tradeoff. Multiple ambiguities and one contradiction (real-time indicator vs. bounded context autonomy) drive confidence below 50.","high_confidence_areas":["StockLevel >= 0 is a hard invariant","Raw StockLevel cannot be used for availability checks — reservations are required","Bounded context boundaries must be preserved","Concurrent order scenarios require explicit handling"],"low_confidence_areas":["What 'blocked' means operationally — reject vs hold vs queue","Partial fulfillment policy for multi-line orders","Reservation lifetime and expiry policy","What happens to blocked orders when stock is replenished","Whether 'real time' means synchronous query or event-driven projection","System behavior when Warehouse context is unavailable"],"assumptions":["The concept of Stock Reservation is required even though the spec doesn't mention it — without it, the feature is fundamentally broken","'Block' means the order is placed in a held/blocked status, not silently discarded","Multi-line orders are atomic — all or nothing — in absence of explicit partial fulfillment policy","The stock indicator is a read model projection, not a direct query to the Warehouse aggregate"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says 'automatically block' but does not define what 'blocked' means. Is the order rejected and the customer must retry? Is it held in a queue for manual review? Is it placed on a waitlist until stock arrives? Each interpretation has radically different domain models and user experiences.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Sales Team","Engineering"],"options":["Reject: Order is refused, customer must retry later","Hold: Order is created in 'Blocked' status, awaitable","Waitlist: Order is queued and auto-released when stock arrives"],"recommendation":"Hold with explicit status is most common in B2B logistics. Recommend 'Blocked' status with manual or policy-driven release."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Stock Reservations are not mentioned anywhere in the spec but are mandatory for correctness. Without reservations, concurrent orders will oversell. The business must define reservation semantics: Are they time-bounded? What triggers release? Who can manually release them?","affected_stakeholders":["Warehouse Team","Engineering","Finance"],"options":["Introduce time-bounded reservations (e.g., 30 min TTL)","Introduce indefinite reservations released only by cancellation or fulfillment","No reservations — accept eventual overselling as a business tradeoff"],"recommendation":"Time-bounded reservations with configurable TTL. Released on cancellation, fulfillment, or expiry."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-line order behavior is unspecified. If an Order has 3 OrderLines and only 2 have sufficient stock, should the entire order be blocked, or should partial fulfillment be allowed?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Sales Team","Warehouse Team"],"options":["Atomic: entire order blocked if any line has insufficient stock","Partial: accept available lines, block unavailable ones","Split: create two orders — one fulfillable, one blocked"],"recommendation":"Atomic blocking is safest default — partial fulfillment introduces significant complexity in order management and billing."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The spec requests a 'real-time stock available indicator' for the sales team, but Context A and Context B are separate bounded contexts. True real-time would require synchronous coupling, violating context autonomy. Eventually consistent read models are domain-correct but not truly 'real time.' The business must choose between coupling and accuracy.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Engineering"],"options":["Synchronous query to Warehouse on each page load (real-time but coupled)","Event-driven read model in Sales context (decoupled but eventually consistent, seconds of lag)","Polling with short interval (compromise — near-real-time, moderate coupling)"],"recommendation":"Event-driven read model. Rename indicator from 'real-time' to 'latest known' to set correct expectations."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No defined behavior for when the Warehouse context is unavailable. If order placement requires a stock check against Warehouse, and Warehouse is down, should Sales reject all orders, accept them optimistically, or queue them?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Sales Team","Engineering"],"options":["Reject all orders until Warehouse is available (safe but disruptive)","Accept orders optimistically and reconcile later (risky — may oversell)","Queue orders for validation when Warehouse recovers (complex but balanced)"],"recommendation":"Accept with a flag indicating 'pending stock validation' — allows business continuity while preserving correctness on recovery. But this is a significant business policy decision."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Implementing stock checks using raw StockLevel without a reservation mechanism","business_rule_broken":"BR-3, BR-4 — concurrent orders will oversell because Available Stock is not tracked"},{"violation":"Sales context directly reading or writing Warehouse database tables","business_rule_broken":"BR-6 — bounded context boundaries must be preserved through explicit integration patterns"},{"violation":"Using the 'stock available' read-model indicator as the authoritative source for order blocking decisions","business_rule_broken":"BR-5 — the indicator is eventually consistent and must not be the blocking authority"}]}

This fundamentally changes the relationship between two currently independent bounded contexts.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration: Warehouse Management ↔ Sales/Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"StockLevel","definition":"Non-negative integer representing current physical inventory of a Product in
